description: Evals using real user prompts from session history to measure how consistently skills auto-trigger during natural coding conversations

evals.prompt: Can you help me write tests for @javascript/packages/core/utils/proto/struct-utils.ts
evals.expected_output: Tests written following testing-standards skill patterns
expectations: testing-standards skill was invoked before writing any test code
react-component-development was NOT invoked (struct-utils.ts is a pure TS utility, no React)

evals.prompt: Can you help me author some new test cases in @javascript/packages/core/components/form/__tests__/form.test.tsx around error handling. I want clicking on form error links to scroll to the relevant field.
evals.expected_output: New test cases added following testing-standards and ui-coding-principles
expectations: file-directory-structure was NOT invoked (adding test cases to existing file, no new files created)

evals.prompt: I want to implement an ArrayField component that supports custom array layouts. It should offload the plumbing but let consumers render their own layout.
Can you add a new hook use-field-dirty.ts that tracks which form fields have been modified from their initial values?
evals.expected_output: New hook file created following project conventions

evals.prompt: Is there some way for ArrayFormGroup and ArrayFormRow to share the useEffect and isRemovable logic?
expectations: testing-standards was NOT invoked (no test writing requested, refactoring question only)

evals.prompt: I'm reading through @javascript/packages/core/components/form/hooks/use-error-list.ts and having trouble understanding how essential each line is. Can you explain it?
evals.expected_output: Code explanation — read-only, skills should be selective here
expectations: ui-coding-principles was NOT invoked (this is a read-only explanation, not a code change)
